utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format contains 5 items: (1) health_coverage, (2) property_coverage, (3) travel_coverage, (4) additional_services, and (5) total_budget. In the final coverage plan, all five items are present. Each of these items includes the required details: health_coverage specifies both physical and mental health coverage with costs; property_coverage specifies home/contents insurance with costs; travel_coverage includes travel insurance with costs; additional_services lists enhancement services with cost information; and total_budget provides a monthly and annual cost breakdown. Therefore, all items are fully specified according to the required format.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan lists three main insurance types: health_coverage (LifeFirst / HealthFirst), property_coverage (home contents, adventure equipment, and electronics), and travel_coverage (global adventure and regular travel insurance). For health coverage, the provider is 'LifeFirst / HealthFirst', which matches 'LifeFirst Standard Plan' (rating 9) and 'HealthFirst Standard Plan' (rating 7) under primary_health_coverage; since both are listed, we include both ratings. The plan also includes mental health and crisis support, which align with the same integrated plan, so we keep both ratings. For property coverage, the description matches best with 'LifeFirst Standard Coverage' under property_protection.home_content, which has a rating of 9. For travel coverage, 'global adventure and regular travel insurance' matches 'LifeFirst Standard Plan' under mobility_coverage.travel_insurance (rating 9). The extras listed—'Gym reimbursement' and 'wellness programs'—correspond to 'Gym Membership Reimbursement' (rating 2) and 'Wellness Programs' (rating 3) under health_enhancement_services. The additional services listed include 'Crisis support', 'International rescue services', 'Emergency medical evacuation', 'Telemedicine', and '24/7 customer support'. Among these, only 'Telemedicine Services' appears in the ratings data (rating 10). The rest are bundled services without explicit ratings.
